title: Bounded Worker Pool, Origin Fairness, Cancellation, and Shutdown · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent community archive receives a different publisher mix, malformed feed, validator state, tenant, schedule, database condition, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers bounded worker pool, origin fairness, cancellation, and shutdown into a changed bounded origin-aware ingestion worker pool. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of bounded origin-aware ingestion worker pool with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress thr…
objectives: Derive global, tenant, origin, database, memory, and queue budgets and enforce the minimum active constraint.
Assign producers, closers, receivers, buffers, and terminal states so sends, closes, and drains cannot race or block forever.
Prevent one publisher from consuming all slots while preserving useful throughput and explicit per-origin limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Go Concurrency Budget
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 6078 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove bounded origin-aware worker ownership, failure isolation, cancellation, and joined shutdown; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Go Concurrency Budget.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for derive global, tenant, origin, database, memory, and queue budgets and enforce the minimum active constraint. For Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Go Concurrency Budget, keep Feed Go Concurrency Budget connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
